commands.review_summary:
The PR requires admin scope or internal authority for Telegram target writeback and threads scope/authority data through Telegram sends, channel sends, cron delivery, CLI sends, and queue replay tests.

PR surface: Source +124, Tests +189. Total +313 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main treats missing `gatewayClientScopes` as allowed for Telegram target writeback, so a scoped-origin path that loses scope metadata can persist config/cron target normalization. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed because the remaining blockers are authorization-sensitive plugin/API direction and real Telegram proof, not a narrow routine repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): require admin for target writeback [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Requires `operator.admin` gateway scope before Telegram target resolution writes back to user config or cron delivery targets.
- Adds explicit internal target-writeback authority for trusted Telegram paths that legitimately do not carry gateway scope metadata.
- Preserves direct Telegram sends, CLI sends, cron delivery, durable sends, action runtime sends, and queued replay while denying scoped non-admin gateway writeback.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes none.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related maintainer-requested hardening for Telegram target writeback authorization.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Gateway-originated Telegram target writeback now requires `operator.admin`; scoped non-admin and missing channel-originated scopes do not persist target normalization, while trusted internal sends use explicit internal authority.
- Real environment tested: Local source checkout and GitHub PR automation; no live Telegram bot token was used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.test-shared.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.storage.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`; `scripts/pr review-tests 88973 extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.test-shared.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.storage.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`; `timeout 1800 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`.
- Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 5 shards; review-pr returned `READY FOR /prepare-pr` with zero findings; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings on head `f10a7f92ad7e6a25a20b20ea3943348f854d5722`.
- Observed result after fix: Missing, empty, and non-admin gateway scopes deny Telegram config/cron writeback; `operator.admin` and explicit internal authority allow trusted writeback.
- What was not tested: Live Telegram API behavior was not exercised.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Behavioral proof uses mocked Telegram/config/cron boundaries.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.test-shared.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.storage.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`
- `scripts/pr review-tests 88973 extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/target-writeback.test-shared.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/cli/send-runtime/channel-outbound-send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.storage.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`
- `timeout 1800 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added target-writeback coverage for missing, empty, non-admin, admin, and explicit internal authority cases. Added propagation coverage for direct Telegram send, action runtime, durable send, CLI send, and queued delivery replay paths.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The previous writeback guard allowed missing scope facts to authorize target persistence. During review, an intermediate patch also regressed direct/internal Telegram send writeback until the internal authority default was moved to the Telegram send boundary.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No schema or migration change. Runtime writeback authorization changes for Telegram target resolution.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Keeping scoped gateway callers separate from trusted internal/non-gateway sends that omit scope metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

The writeback sink requires admin scope or explicit internal authority, durable channel-originated replies default missing scopes to an empty list, and focused tests cover gateway-scoped and internal paths separately.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for GitHub Actions and maintainer review on head `f10a7f92ad7e6a25a20b20ea3943348f854d5722`.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub Actions are pending for the latest pushed head.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed structured review findings about preserving trusted internal target writeback while failing closed for gateway-scoped non-admin callers.
